title: willemetia stipitata subsp. stipitata
intro: Willemetia stipitata subsp. stipitata, also known as the Maori potato or kāuri kūmara, is a subspecies of the Willemetia stipitata plant, a type of yam native to New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Willemetia stipitata subsp. stipitata include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared as a herbal tea or used in tincture form, with its roots often being consumed raw or cooked, highlighting its versatility in traditional Maori medicine.
